extraction.one_line_summary: BETTIOL Alberto attacked from 23-rider break on final climb, dropped CAVAGNA on penultimate ascent, won solo by 17 seconds
tactical_patterns: CAVAGNA's early attack on penultimate climb forced selection but left him completely empty on final ascent.
BETTIOL chose to bridge gradually rather than follow CAVAGNA's initial move, saving energy for final climb acceleration.
ROOCH showed superior pacing discipline by letting CAVAGNA go, closing to within 20 seconds but never truly threatening.

rider_signals.BETTIOL Alberto: positive: Classics rider displaying exceptional climbing form, attacking decisively on category climbs rather than just following
rider_signals.CAVAGNA Remi: negative: Strong enough to create selection but tactical error going too early left him completely depleted
rider_signals.ROOCH Nicholas: positive: Intelligent chase earned podium but couldn't close final 20 seconds despite CAVAGNA fading
rider_signals.CONSONNI Simone: positive: Unexpected second place from advancing group suggests form peak; unusual result for sprinter-type on this terrain
